definition: Global crop balance sheets are in a 'thinning buffer' phase — corn stocks-to-use ex-China below 15%, wheat ~32% (down from ~35%), soybeans ~27% — placing the system on the convex portion of the price-stocks curve where the next weather disruption triggers disproportionate price responses. The FX transmission runs through EM food inflation → rate differential widening → carry stress.
evidence: Balance sheet data publicly verifiable from USDA WASDE
Convex price-stocks relationship well-established in agricultural economics (Wright 2011, Cafiero et al. 2011)
FX transmission chain: thinning buffers → smaller weather shock needed → EM food inflation → rate widening → carry stress
open_questions: System can persist in thin-buffer state for multiple seasons if weather cooperates
Whether precision agriculture and yield improvements are offsetting buffer thinning
What specific stocks-to-use threshold triggers the convex price response
